betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6No Tree Punching§f

§aUnlearn standard survival mechanics and construct primitive tools to conquer the early game!§f
The §9No Tree Punching§f mod brings high-realism overhaul mechanics to your early days. Forget punching trees for logs or breaking stone with bare hands; attempting this will yield no resources. Instead, you must forage the ground for loose rocks and knap sharp flint shard tools to carve out your survival.

§6Primitive Tooling & Knapping§f:
§b• Loose Rock Gathering§f: Gather stone, marble, limestone, and basalt rocks lying loose on the ground by §9right-clicking§f them.
§b• Flint Knapping§f: Find gravel, extract flint, and §9right-click§f a hard stone block with §bFlint§f in your hand. There is a §c60% chance§f of starting a knapping attempt and a §a70% success rate§f to shatter it into sharp §bFlint Shards§f.
§b• Plant Fibers§f: Craft a §bFlint Knife§f to cut tall grass. Breaking tall grass with a knife has a §a40% chance§f to drop §bPlant Fibers§f, which can be crafted into string for binding tools.
§b• Log Chopping§f: Craft a §bFlint Hatchet§f to process logs. Logs cannot be placed into a crafting grid to make planks. Instead, place logs on the ground and §9right-click§f the top face with a Hatchet or Axe for a §a60% chance§f to chop them into planks.

§6Primitive Infrastructure§f:
§b• The Fire Pit§f: A primitive clay cookstove. Cook times are §c400 ticks (20s)§f — twice as slow as a furnace. Fuel burns §c5× faster§f than a furnace and is capped at a maximum of §c800 ticks (40s)§f of burn capacity per item. You can §9right-click§f torches onto a lit fire pit to light them.
§b• Fire Starter§f: A friction-based starter with a §c50% chance§f to successfully light fires.
§b• Ceramic Bucket§f: An early-game clay bucket. Unlike iron, ceramic buckets are fragile and can §aonly carry water§f (carrying lava or other high-temperature fluids is disabled).

§6NTP Tool Material Tiers§f:
§b• Tin§f: Level §e0§f (wood tier equivalent).
§b• Flint & Copper§f: Level §e1§f (stone tier equivalent).
§b• Bronze§f: Level §e2§f (iron tier equivalent).
§b• Steel§f: Level §e3§f (diamond tier equivalent).

§6Your First Day Checklist§f:
1. §bForage the Forest Floor§f: Walk around and §9right-click§f loose rocks on the ground, then break leaves with your hands to gather sticks (leaves have a §a20% chance§f to drop a stick).
2. §bShape the Flint§f: Dig gravel to get flint, and §9right-click§f it on solid stone blocks to knap flint shards. Combine a flint shard, a stick, and plant fiber string to craft a Flint Knife.
3. §bChop Wood§f: Use your knife on grass to obtain fibers, craft a Flint Hatchet, place logs on the ground, and §9right-click§f their top faces with the hatchet to claim your first planks.
